message:
beads: sync issue tracker with pane performance and feature planning updates

Update beads issue tracking to reflect current state of pane interaction
features, performance budgets, and profiling work items. Adds 38 new
issue entries covering pane-perf budgets, drag latency counters, and
solver cost optimization targets.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"bd-2enu3","title":"bug(frankenterm-web): harden wasm init + renderer robustness","description":"Fix correctness issues in frankenterm-web wasm bindings + WebGPU renderer:\n\n- init option parsing: reject NaN/non-integers instead of truncating/casting to 0\n- render(): error when renderer not initialized (avoid silent no-op)\n- destroy(): fully reset modifier/IME/composition + encoder state for reuse\n- WebGPU atlas sub-rect uploads: pad bytes_per_row to 256 alignment\n- Surface get_current_texture errors: for Lost/Outdated, reconfigure + retry once","notes":"Implemented wasm init strict numeric parsing + render/destroy guards; fixed WebGPU dirty atlas bytes_per_row 256 alignment and surface Lost/Outdated retry. Fixed failing mermaid JSONL tests (layout_metrics expectation). Pending: workspace cargo test blocked by bd-2yjus program.rs test compile break (MaroonCliff reservation).

New repro from user (mac Chrome + Safari): garbled/noisy grid rendering + slow first paint; console error \"missing required string field: key\" in FrankenTerm.js.

Likely causes/fixes:
1) crates/frankenterm-web/src/renderer.rs: GpuRenderer::ensure_glyph_slot still uses rasterize_procedural_glyph for cache misses; wire GlyphCanvasRasterizer::rasterize instead.
2) crates/frankenterm-web/src/wasm.rs: parse_key_event currently requires key/code strings via get_string; make tolerant of undefined (use get_string_opt + default), and/or coerce in frankentui_showcase_demo.html with String(e.key ?? \"\") / String(e.code ?? \"\"), and capture keys on window for parity.

---
Update 2026-02-10 (EmeraldForge)
- Root cause for blank/garbled initial render: host HTML did not apply the initial full repaint patch batch produced by ShowcaseRunner.init(); first step can be rendered=false and/or overwrite last_patches.
- Fix: after runner.init(), immediately runner.takeFlatPatches() -> term.applyPatchBatchFlat(...) -> term.render().
- Added cache-busting query params for JS/WASM imports to avoid Cloudflare/browser stale assets.
- Deployed Cloudflare Pages: https://frankentui-showcase-demo.pages.dev/ (preview: https://f3bfb30f.frankentui-showcase-demo.pages.dev/)
- Commit: a47f8215

 {"id":"bd-2vr05.14.4","title":"task(progress): implement deterministic progress signaling API and OSC-mapped semantics","description":"Background\n- xterm.js progress addon usage depends on exposing terminal-originated progress signals to host UI.\n- FrankenTermJS should provide a first-party deterministic progress event surface without addon coupling.\n\nScope\n- Define supported progress signal sources (including mapped escape-sequence forms) and normalization rules.\n- Expose typed progress events with ordering guarantees and bounded delivery semantics.\n- Integrate with host API docs and capability flags.\n\nAcceptance\n- Equivalent input/replay streams produce identical progress event sequences.\n- Unsupported/prohibited signals fail deterministically with traceable diagnostics.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria\n- Progress signal taxonomy and normalization rules are explicit, deterministic, and versioned.\n- Unit tests cover sequence ordering, dedupe/coalescing policy, invalid signal handling, and capability gating.\n- Browser E2E scripts validate host-visible progress lifecycle behavior with structured JSONL diagnostics.\n- API contract tests ensure typed event payload stability and error semantics.\n\n## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate\n- Comprehensive unit tests are required for implementation and edge-case regressions.\n- Deterministic E2E scripts are required for impacted workflows and must run in CI.\n- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (timestamps, correlation IDs, failure context) sufficient for deterministic replay.","notes":"Execution Notes\n- Keep progress events in the same deterministic ordering model as other host events.\n- Do not leak unbounded high-frequency progress updates;  {"id":"bd-2vr05.14.5","title":"task(text-render): implement optional ligature/shaping path with deterministic fallback","description":"Background\n- Many xterm.js users depend on ligature rendering for code readability in modern fonts.\n- FrankenTermJS should support this as an explicit, optional capability without sacrificing deterministic core behavior.\n\nScope\n- Add optional ligature/shaping render path with stable feature flags and fallback to canonical grapheme rendering.\n- Define cache/invalidation behavior for shaped clusters and font changes.\n- Ensure selection/cursor/extraction semantics remain correct when ligature shaping is enabled.\n\nAcceptance\n- Enabling/disabling ligature mode produces predictable, documented rendering differences only.\n- Core editing/navigation semantics remain invariant across ligature settings.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria\n- Ligature/shaping mode is capability-gated, deterministic, and documented with explicit fallback semantics.\n- Unit tests cover shaping cache invalidation, cursor/selection boundary correctness, and copy/extract invariants under ligature mode.\n- Browser E2E scripts validate ligature-enabled workflows across representative fonts with structured JSONL logs.\n- Performance and memory impacts are benchmarked and gated to prevent regressions.\n\n## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate\n- Comprehensive unit tests are required for implementation and edge-case regressions.\n- Deterministic E2E scripts are required for impacted workflows and must run in CI.\n- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (timestamps, correlation IDs, failure context) sufficient for deterministic replay.","notes":"Execution Notes\n- Treat ligatures as a rendering concern;  {"id":"bd-2vr05.2","title":"feature(frankenterm-js): websocket attach client and lifecycle robustness","description":"Background
- A terminal emulator is not production-usable on the web without robust attach lifecycle logic to connect browser UI with remote PTY streams.
- xterm.js deployments commonly depend on mature websocket glue. FrankenTermJS must ship an equivalent first-party path without legacy baggage.

Goal
- Deliver a deterministic, bounded, and protocol-compliant websocket attach client for FrankenTermJS.

In Scope
- Client state machine from disconnected to connected to teardown.
- Binary protocol envelope codec with strict validation.
- Flow-control windows and explicit backpressure behavior.
- Reconnect and resume semantics with clear guarantees.
- End-to-end harness coverage against ftui-remote bridge.

Out of Scope
- Ad hoc plugin-based attach behavior.
- Best-effort unbounded buffering under overload.

Why This Matters
- Without this, remote mode remains demo-only.
- This track is required for practical replacement of xterm.js in real web apps.

Acceptance Criteria
- Browser attaches to ftui-remote and supports interactive shell workflows under load.
- Protocol errors are deterministic and diagnosable.
- Memory and queue behavior remain bounded during output bursts.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Feature behavior is validated by comprehensive unit tests that cover core invariants and edge cases.
- Feature behavior is validated by end-to-end test scripts that exercise realistic user workflows.
- E2E runs emit detailed structured logs (JSONL or equivalent) sufficient for deterministic failure triage and replay.
- CI gates for this feature fail on regression in correctness, determinism, or declared standards scope.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:33.956536552Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:03:15.417575547Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenterm","protocol","remote","websocket"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:33.956536552Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:49:03.229894445Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

- Validation output must include structured logs detailed enough for deterministic postmortem analysis.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
